reviews.review_text:
I’ve been going to Currarong Caravan Park since I was a child, and it’s honestly a relief to finally see the previous management removed. The level of unprofessionalism from Alex and Bec was unbelievable. On two separate occasions Alex walked into our tent swearing aggressively in front of women and children. The second time I stood up for myself, he retaliated by calling police and falsely claiming I assaulted him. After a long and stressful process, the matter went nowhere. I reported this to the shoalhaven city council park management and they completely ignored my report.

What’s even worse is Shoalhaven City Council completely failed in its duty of care and due diligence toward long-term residents and visitors. The way this situation was handled was disgraceful and reflected broader mismanagement within the council itself.

Hopefully with new management in place the park can finally return to what it used to be, and hopefully the ongoing incompetence and failures within Shoalhaven City Council are finally exposed for everyone to see.
Like that Karate Kid, Daniel-son; the best
Adored this holiday park. Was absolutely brilliant for the kids
Nice burgers
Very well operated and very well positioned. The beach and inlet are awesome for all ages. The park was clean and everyone was very friendly. We’ve booked for next year already.
worst camping ever. people played club music even after 11, 12pm kept shouting by playing games. No one manages this quiet time.

Currently staying over Christmas at Holiday haven and it has been fantastic . We have booked 3 big sites for our 3 families and we have loved every minute .
Great customer service
Great Cleanliness in all the amenities (at different times of the day too ) including the pool , toilets and the whole park
Great showers pressure is awesome !!!!
Great location so close to the beach/lake and walking distance to the rock pools
We will be rebooking for next year again
Being over Xmas too we were worried about the noise and space BUT it’s been so quiet everyone has been well behaved and we have lots of space doesn’t feel cramped at all the park is very well spaced out .
Thankyou holiday haven
From the callus/harthill-laws and Studmans

As another guest from Giaan’s group, and given we cannot respond directly to the owner’s reply, I feel it is necessary to clarify a few points for “accuracy”. It is disappointing that your reply focuses primarily on disputing our experience rather than acknowledging it.
Whether our group has stayed once or multiple times previously does not change the facts of this booking, nor does it lessen the expectation that paid sites would not be altered without notice. We booked and paid for specific ensuite sites, which were changed without any prior communication. This was only discovered upon arrival, leaving no opportunity to prepare or adjust plans. Notably, one of our original paid sites remained vacant, yet relocation was refused.
While you reference your terms and conditions, policies do not negate the importance of basic communication, accountability, or customer care, particularly when errors occur. A simple explanation or acknowledgment would have gone a long way in de-escalating the situation.
With regard to the conduct of staff, our concerns were raised calmly and respectfully. Being told to leave the office, hearing staff swear under their breath and being threatened with removal despite no abusive behaviour was confronting and unnecessary. These actions contributed significantly to the distress of five families, particularly during what was meant to be a holiday period.
You state that race or ethnicity was not mentioned during interactions. However, the perception of differential treatment arose from observable actions, including the reallocation of our original sites to others while our group was denied relocation. While intent may be disputed, impact remains, and dismissing this concern outright rather than reflecting on it only reinforces why the experience felt so upsetting.
We acknowledge that peak periods are challenging to manage. However, professionalism is demonstrated not by rigidly standing behind policies, but by how issues are handled when things go wrong. Unfortunately, that was not reflected in our experience.
We maintain our review as an accurate account of what occurred and hope future guests receive clearer communication and more considerate resolution should similar issues arise.

We’ve stayed at Holiday Haven Currarong every Christmas for years without issue, but this year was extremely disappointing.

We booked and paid for our ensuite sites, however, they were changed without notice or consent. We only found out that the change had occurred when checking in on the day. Although one of our paid original sites were vacant, management refused to relocated it to us. The only alternative offered were two ensuites far away from our sites, that found to be useless in pitching our tents as they were caravan sites. After hours of struggling to pitch the tents, we had to cram 5 tents and 2 gazebos into 3 camp sites due to their error in providing appropriate sites for tents.

Staff member Kelly claimed she was the “victim” of verbal attacks, despite us only raising concerns and complaining of the sudden changes. The manager, Rebecca, refused to take responsibility or apologize. We observed that our original paid sites were reallocated to white guests, while our group, who are Asian, were refused relocation. Kelly also swore quietly as we were yelled at to leave the office, and management threatened to forcibly remove all five families despite no abusive behaviour. The customer service from the management and admin team was absolutely appalling, unprofessional, irresponsible, dismissive, coercive, rude and discriminatory/racist against Asians.

There was:
• No prior notification of the site change
• No explanation for this action
• No accountability taken by management
• No apology for their actions

After many years of returning, this experience caused significant disappointment amongst 5 families and felt unfairly targeted. Future guests should be aware that site allocation errors may not be remedied appropriately, and that racism  and discriminatory treatments may be subjected at certain ethnicities, especially Asians.

Holiday Haven was just such a great caravan park, the family who run it were just outstanding and so were the staff!  We made a mistake with our booking and they were so accommodating and really helped us out! We're travelling full time and don't often leave reviews but had to review this awesome park! Splash pad for kids, pool, playground,mini golf,carts you can ride around on, close to the beach! Thanks guys !!

Great amenities at the park, splash park, pool, jumping pillow and basketball court - all fabulous. ||So good that we could bring our dog. ||Cabins and camping areas around the edge of park were great. ||Easy check-in/out. ||Beware - Approximately 80% of the park is made up of permanent, rundown, old and dingy caravans/with annexes - this gave the park a rough vibe. Groups hanging around in these areas just staring. I would not let younger kids wander the park without an adult, even though kids wanted to. ||Not much at Currarong, if you are looking for a more upbeat vibe, go to Huskisson.
Putt putt Is one of the park activities
Awesome van Park. Easy walk to beach and local bowling club. Was lucky enough to have a site with zero neighbours but would probably get busy during silly season

Very nice large park with modern toilets/showers. Lots of permanent vans.
Only problem, no local general store, so you'll need to bring all food or go on trips to Nowra etc.
Bowling club 2 min walk.
Great park. Managers friendly and helpful. Dog friendly. Plenty of room even for larger vans. Clean amenities,very well maintained. Can't wait to go back.

We stayed in a sea breeze cabin which was clean and very comfortable with ample space. We had friends staying in other cabins that also commented the same. The park is in a beautiful place and it was refreshing to stay in a holiday park where the staff seemed to genuinely enjoy hosting you. We will definitely be back.
Nice park - Cabins are clean, modern and well priced. Definitely recommend this for anyone looking for a weekend away. Beach and local area are nice also.

Very disappointing experience. We were locked inside (more than 10 ppl at the time) the campground playground at night with no way to exit – both the entry and exit gates were shut with no notice or way to open them, code or buttom don't work. There was no one at reception to assist, and we had to climb over a gate just to get out. This is a serious safety issue and completely unacceptable. Clear signage, emergency access, or after-hours support should be in place to prevent situations like this.
A lovely caravan park. Quiet in mid May. The ensuite was excellent..
Perfect location, really great ensuite sites perfect for families. Staff of the park were great too. Was a super busy long weekend in the park but was still a peaceful stay! Will be back again soon!

Location is spot on, lots of beaches and exploring to do. Park is well maintained and tidy. Cabin tidy and functional for 2A 2K. Good amount of activities in park.
Only issue with service is due to a family member being treated rudly regarding the camp spot for their forward fold trailer ( just confirm what size your trailer is!!). Would recommend!

My wife and I stayed on a non powered site last weekend and had a great experience. The managers, Alex and Bec made us feel very much at home and offered excellent advice about the area. The amenities are modern, clean and abundant. We could not have asked for more!

Beautiful spot and well maintained park only thing I found annoying was having to punch in a code for everything. The tap and pay laundry experience was a game changer, why isnt everyone doing this?! Overall staff were friendly and accommodating and we would highly recommend.

This caravan park is huge & absolutely geared towards kids & families!
So if you are looking for a peaceful place to go for a quiet weekend, this is definitely not the place to go.
It’s so loud during the day - bikes, electric scooters & bikes (even though they are definitely not allowed in the park, they most certainly are there), dogs barking. People everywhere!
I think that, for me, it was the dogs that barked during the night. I am a light sleeper… so needless to say I hard a tough time.

Sorry, but we won’t be back.
Stayed there for the first time 20 years ago and not much has changed in the environment. The amenities are clean and staff are helpful. Wake up to birds singing sounds of surf.
A nice park in a quiet, out of the way location. Facilities all good, nice pool and splash pad which my children loved. We only stayed one night but could have easily done more.

Perfect place to take young kids! It has a splash park, jumping pillow, big swimming pool, and walking distance to the beach and Currarong River. Our cabin was perfect for my family (with two kids under 5) with a great kitchen, cool bunk beds, and a BBQ very close by. I would highly recommend for anyone looking for a cost-effective family summer holiday, just need to book fairly early in advance due to its popularity!

Really enjoyed this park. Super clean. Friendly staff. Recreation areas were fabulous and well maintained. Super clear instructions and great communication throughout our stay. School holiday activities for the kids were an added bonus to our stay.
We had a great week staying at the holiday haven currarong. The facilities are great and very clean, only moments to the beach and lots of great walks. Perfect place for a relaxing summer holiday!
The staff are very nice and friendly, and the facilities are excellent.

Our summer 2024 stay was marvellous. The setting was beautiful, amenities generous and staff accomodating. Pool and play area was a fav of the kids, but our proximity to beaches and walks kept us busy with relaxing out of the cabin. The grounds were incredibly neat and clean with a small army of attentive staff ensuring the park was tip top. We plan to be back soon.
Beautiful day in this part of the world
Great little campsite in an awesome location. The beach and creek are lovely for swim. Cute cafes in the area and very chilled compared to other towns in Jervis Bay. I’ll be back.
Beautiful place and very nice location

Where do I start . |Well during a serious weather event  the staff looked after us ten fold .  The place is absolutely  professionally  ran , with organising events  on special weekends to holidays . We stayed there twice for over a month each time   |Both times excellent  service exceeding the standards  |I'd personally  recommend  staying there  A1 experience
Stayed in swag non powered. Great location. Kids loved it. Stayed an extra night. Staff very good. Very clean amenities. Beautiful beach and bush walks
Incredible caravan site with great powered sites, facilities and location. We’ll be back for sure.

Great park, very family friendly. They have gate access to the beach, the boat wash facilities are a special extra we weren’t anticipating but loved. The ensuite site is so modern and clean I loved it! The Sites are very close together so privacy and quiet time is a little challenging. The amenities block is basic and a little tired in comparison to the ensuite but very clean and has everything you need. So grateful to the staff for easy check in and a fabulous experience !

We camped for two nights at this well kept park.
After our first night of nearby noisy campers, Bec assisted us to a quieter site.
She could not have done more for us.
She assisted with site relocation and sent constant check ins to ensure we enjoyed our stay.
Great location to the beach and the Peninsula Brassiere is walking distance also and a great feed.
Would definitely recommend
Great place

Amazing place to stay. Very comfortable self contained cabins. Very well stocked and maintained. Great for the kids. Very close to the beach. The weather was terrible but we still had a great time. Would highly recommend stayimg there.

School reunion. A great camp kitchen area. Plenty of sites for vans. Clean toilets. Close to beach and a beautiful area to explore. A great place to spend some time. Holiday Haven has cabins for hire and sites for RVs,  caravan and camping... not a hotel.
We stayed in a new ensuite site. It was spotless. The location, facilities and staff were excellent. Can’t wait to get back.
Can't recommend this park enough. We are organising our wedding down there atm and they can't help us enough. Nothing is ever too much trouble and the staff are beautiful 😍

Ja abgelegen aber das war o.k.
Freundlicher Empfang.
Was uns nicht gefallen hat waren die eng stehenden Unterkünfte der "Dauercamper" da war wenig Charme.

Unfortunately we couldn’t have our stay at the park because my car broke down a hundred km before. But when I called them to cancel my reservation on the same day they were very friendly and they gave me a refund without any hesitation and this helped me a lot in my unlucky situation.
Great place so close to beach

Love this park, we had 3 cabins with very close access to the beach and all the facilities, so safe for the children too, we have rebooked to come back next year, that will be our 3rd year, can't wait!  Also many thanks to Rebecca on Reception who was so lovely and helpful :)

We just left today after a 5 night stay in our caravan. Clean, tidy and quiet park large size sites available. The park has a jumping pillow, pool and warer park. Literally 100 metre walk to the lovely beach. Kids loved it. Would definitely stay again.
Nice place family friendly

We stayed here three weeks ago for the Callala triathlon. Beach nearby, as well as an on-site pool, and some fun things for kids. There's a little camp kitchen for us campers, with a fridge/freezer and oven.
Every time we needed something, Sarah on reception was awesome at helping us out. Always greeting us with a beautiful smile, she was so knowledgeable that I couldn't believe she'd been working there less than a week!
We had such a good time that we've booked to stay here again in February for the Husky Triathlon Festival, and we'll be staying for a few days longer to really appreciate the surrounding area.

One thing to note is that the code for the boom gate expires at 10.30am on the date of departure. It's also the code for the toilet block too, so be prepared for that!

Caravan power site. Online check in easy access. Coming here for over 25 years. Same site but from non- powered updated to power, dump point etc, new bitumen roadway and raised ground levelling. Unfortunately, once level sites with soil settling has meant poor water run off from site(s) to roadway drainage which handles well. Advised new manager of a few months that great works have not followed up with filling in many areas especially where annexed are on sites. Although unusual weather this week - flooding is caused by lack of followup levelling and will get worst during peak season. Hope this is rectified otherwise many would not rebook. Great beach in easy walking distance, good boating/fishing, easy beautiful walks, neat Currarong Bowling Club (kid friendly), bistro good variety (love the squid) but something for all including pool tables, darts, bowls and more. Worth a visit or holiday at Currarong NSW about 20km from Nowra on the coast - easy drive.
There website is accurate. Great staff and enjoyed the experience 👍

Quiet location , very clean environment good walking & beaches
Worth a visit
Not the best caravan park I’ve been too. Although I don’t have many complaints. The only bad thing is the surrounding area. The closest proper supermarket is 25 minutes away..
Great facilities for the kids. Park has a jumping pillow which are disappearing from caravan parks.

Stayed 7 nights.

Site: large site with a bit of dirt, which didn’t bother us too much. Been to lots of places worse and could easily pay a little more for Astroturf if needed / wanted. Site was clean and well kept.

Staff: there aren’t a lot of caravan parks we have been to where the staff actually enjoy working there. Every staff member was delightful. The family who are running it at the moment are fantastic.

Location: This park is central to some absolutely stunning spots. Currarong rock pools, Culbura, Callala, honeymoon bay to name a few. Perfect spot for us for the week.

Showers: I wish I could take the heat, pressure, space of these showers for the rest of the trip. After 4 weeks around Wollongong with absolute trash showers these have restored my faith.

Additional: putt putt and pool are fantastic. Both well kept and the kids enjoyed them.

The place was a ghost town Mon-Thurs. felt like the only people here. This was great. Like most parks near Sydney, the weekend crew rolled in and yet still a great vibe.
Good for family
Love camping here for the last 15 years and many more to come have now booked twice a year now
